user_query:
Build a complete thesis-first trade plan for AAPL using only the provided files. Read all inputs under input/ and produce four artifacts under output/:
1) output/thesis.yaml — a filled Thesis Brief using the methodology: include edge type and description, a one-sentence thesis statement, timeframe, bull/base/bear scenarios with probabilities that sum to 100, target prices, expected_value and ev_vs_current vs current_price, and clear invalidation (price_stop, thesis_stop, time_stop). Keep conviction 1-5 and list at least two supporting factors sourced from the input files.
2) output/position_size.json — compute a position for AAPL using account/risk constraints from input/portfolio.json: position size must respect both max 2% risk per trade and max 10% single-position weight. Include: account_equity, risk_per_trade_percent, entry_price, stop_loss_price, risk_per_share, position_size_units, position_value, risk_amount, portfolio_weight_percent, and boolean checks for within_risk_limit, within_position_weight_limit, and within_portfolio_heat_limit (see #4).
3) output/trade_journal.json — open-trade entry following the template: id, date_opened, ticker, direction, entry_price, stop_loss, at least two take-profit targets, computed risk_reward (string like "1:3.0" or a numeric ratio field), thesis summary, edge_type, conviction, entry_type, and placeholders for exit fields. Ensure risk_reward is at least 1:2.
4) output/risk_report.json — a portfolio risk summary after adding this AAPL position: include detected market regime from input/market_regime.yaml, pre_trade_portfolio_heat_percent and post_trade_portfolio_heat_percent (must not exceed 15%), max_single_position_breach (boolean), correlated_exposure_ok (boolean) and correlated_exposure_combined_weight for the most correlated existing holding from input/correlations.json, plus a short note on any mitigation if correlated exposure is near limits.

Ground rules:
- Do not fetch external data. Use only the numbers and hints in input/.
- Use the current_price in input/briefing.md for EV and sizing, and align all calculations to that number.
- If technicals in input/technicals.json imply a bearish or low-quality setup, size more conservatively but still comply with the hard limits (≤2% risk, ≤10% weight, ≤15% portfolio heat) and document why in the thesis and risk_report.
- Write all outputs to the specified paths under output/ and keep file formats exactly as requested (YAML/JSON).
